The `deploy_edge_function` `files` array must include every `_shared/*.ts` dependency, not just the new one. Ref: `_shared/eligibility.ts` shared by `get-listing-detail` (v4) + `apply-to-listing` (v2).
113	- **`KeyboardAvoidingView` is unreliable inside an RN `<Modal>` (and therefore inside our `BottomSheet`).** The Modal opens its own native window, so KAV's measured frame and the keyboard's reported frame disagree and the contents do not get pushed up. Replace KAV with a direct `Keyboard.addListener('keyboardWillShow' / 'keyboardWillHide')` (use `keyboardDidShow`/`keyboardDidHide` on Android) that drives `paddingBottom` on the inner `<View>` from `e.endCoordinates.height`. Remember that `BottomSheet` snap point must accommodate `keyboard_height (~336px iOS) + content_height` — for the apply sheet that meant raising `snapPoints` from `[0.7]` to `[0.85]`. Ref: `src/screens/listing-detail/ApplySheet.tsx`.
114	- **Defer `router.replace`/`router.push` after dismissing a `BottomSheet` by ~350ms** so the sheet's spring-out animation (`SPRING_SNAPPY` settles in ~300ms — see `src/design/motion.ts`) plays cleanly before the new screen mounts. Without the delay, the sheet slides down on top of the freshly-rendered destination, which looks broken. The same delay applies to any imperative navigation triggered from a Modal/BottomSheet success path. Ref: `app/(creator)/listing/[id].tsx` `onApplied`.
115	- **Run cheap checks before upstream oEmbed/scraper calls in edge functions; return `502 OEMBED_UNAVAILABLE` (not 500) when the upstream itself fails.** Order inside an endpoint that relies on a third-party URL validator: JWT → role → parse body → regex-classify URL → DB lookups → status/permission gates → affirmation/eligibility check → upstream oEmbed → RPC. This is both a cost optimization (don't burn TikTok rate-limit slots on requests that will 4xx regardless) and a DDoS-amplifier guard (an unauth POST must not be able to force an upstream fetch). Split the error codes: `500 SERVER_MISCONFIGURED` / `500 DB_ERROR` for our-fault failures, `502 OEMBED_UNAVAILABLE` for upstream unavailability — clients that auto-retry benefit because upstream outages are usually retryable, DB bugs rarely are. Document BOTH codes in the contract comment. Ref: `supabase/functions/submit-video/index.ts` oEmbed section + error envelope header.
116	- **Defensive null-guards on NOT NULL columns fetched via service-role client, when the row feeds a security-relevant gate.** The database schema can enforce NOT NULL, but a TypeScript read from a future mis-migrated row that returns `null` will silently propagate into downstream logic. When the value gates a security check (e.g. `application.listing_version_id` → the post-condition affirmation check; an empty `.eq('listing_version_id', null)` fetch returns zero rows → no affirmations required → gate bypassed), add an explicit `if (!row.field) { 500 DB_ERROR }`. One extra branch is cheaper than a future exploitable silent-pass. Ref: `supabase/functions/submit-video/index.ts` listing_version_id guard.
117	- **PostgREST embeds silently NULL joined columns when the target table's RLS filters the caller out — always audit every `.select('..., related!fk(col)')` embed against the related table's RLS policies.** Failure mode: the embed syntax succeeds, returns rows, and populates the parent columns; the embedded field just comes back as `null` or `[]` under RLS because PostgREST applies the policies per-row transparently. Highest-risk embeds on this project: (a) `users!lister_id(username)` where `users_self_select` is self-only — every cross-tenant handle read (creator viewing lister handle, lister viewing applicant handle) will null out; (b) `listing_versions!...(...)` where `listing_versions_read_if_listing_readable` gates on `listings.status='active' OR caller=lister` — any historical/closed listing version the caller doesn't own will null; (c) `listings!...(title)` where `listings_public_read` requires `status='active'` — same closed-listing nullout. Rule: before writing a PostgREST embed, check the target table's RLS; if the policy wouldn't return the specific row under the caller's role, replace the embed with a SECURITY DEFINER RPC that does its own ownership check. DO NOT widen the self-only policy to "return usernames if handle is public" — that leaks the full row shape; DEFINER + narrow column projection is the correct primitive. Ref: Codebase Pattern #40 (`submission_reuse_count`), Codebase Pattern #110 (`apply_to_listing_rpc`), and `public.list_my_applications()` from US-043.
118	- **SECURITY DEFINER "list-my-rows-with-foreign-context" RPC skeleton.** Contract: `returns table(<flat columns including the cross-tenant bits>)`, `language sql stable security definer set search_path = ''`, body is a straight `select ... from public.<owned_table> t left join public.<foreign_table> f on ... where t.<owner_col> = public.current_user_id() order by ...`. Grants: `revoke all ... from public`, `revoke all ... from anon`, `grant execute ... to authenticated`. Service-role is intentionally not granted — it already has BYPASSRLS and wouldn't use this shape. Why `stable`, not `volatile`: the function reads but doesn't write, so PostgREST can plan around it and query planner caches can reuse results within a statement. Why `sql` over `plpgsql`: the body is one statement, no control flow, no GET STACKED DIAGNOSTICS — inlines better. Verified pattern via a transactional `begin; set local role authenticated; set local "request.jwt.claims" = '{"sub":"<uuid>","role":"creator"}'; select * from public.<fn>();` block that confirms (a) the DEFINER bypass returns the cross-tenant columns, (b) the ownership check still gates other users' rows (swap the sub claim to a different creator and assert zero rows). Ref: `supabase/migrations/20260418010100_us_043_list_my_applications_rpc.sql`. Applies to any future "my X with lister/creator/reviewer context" list screen.
119	- **Screen-RPC naming convention: `public.list_my_<things>` for caller-scoped list reads, `public.<action>_rpc` for mutations, `public.<thing>_<metric>(arg)` for aggregates.** Precedents: `public.list_my_applications()` (US-043) / `public.apply_to_listing_rpc(...)` (US-041) / `public.submission_reuse_count(uuid)` (US-009). Keep the `list_my_*` functions argumentless (the caller is implicit via `current_user_id()`) — passing a caller UUID would re-introduce the spoofing surface the helpers exist to eliminate. Keep them in the `public` schema (not `app_private`) because they need to be callable via PostgREST `supabase.rpc(...)`; `app_private` is reserved for server-side triggers + cron bodies. The `authenticated` grant is mandatory — without it PostgREST returns 42501 "permission denied for function" and the MCP `execute_sql` test path (runs as `postgres`) silently passes, masking the bug.
120	- **Segmented control a11y: `accessibilityRole="tablist"` wrapper + `accessibilityRole="tab"` on each button + `accessibilityState={{ selected }}`**, NOT the Chip primitive's `role="checkbox"` + `checked`. Chip is for multi-select filter rows where each pill is independent; a segmented control is single-select and semantically is a tab bar. iOS VoiceOver reads "tab, Pending, selected, 1 of 4" for the tablist shape — correct under Apple's a11y guidance. `selected` is the right state (not `checked`) because the user isn't toggling a boolean, they're picking one of N mutually-exclusive views. Inline the control rather than promoting to a shared primitive until a second screen needs it — premature abstraction hurts more than a ~40-line duplicate. Ref: `SegmentControl` in `app/(creator)/applications.tsx` (US-043).
121	- **Enum-to-UI-bucket maps live as a static `Record<DBEnum, UISegment>` next to the screen that owns the mapping, not in a shared constants file.** `application_status` has 6 values; the Applied tab shows 4 segments (pending / approved / rejected / cancelled), with `withdrawn + cancelled_listing_edit + cancelled_listing_closed` all bucketing to "cancelled" per design.md §3.1 label + the AC's 4-segment design. Type the map as `Record<Database['public']['Enums']['application_status'], Segment>` so adding a 7th enum value trips tsc at the map literal. Do NOT collapse the three cancellation variants in the DB (they carry distinct semantic meaning for future admin/analytics use); collapse only at the UI layer. Same pattern applies to any status enum whose DB shape is richer than the screen's tab count. Ref: `STATUS_BUCKET` in `app/(creator)/applications.tsx`.
122	
123	---
124	
125	## 2026-04-18 00:38 — US-043
126	- New screen `app/(creator)/applications.tsx` (~290 lines) replaces the placeholder: `<SafeAreaView>` → `<ScrollView>` → "Applied" display header → inline `SegmentControl` (4 pills: Pending / Approved / Rejected / Cancelled, accessibilityRole='tab' + accessibilityState={selected}) → content area. Content states: loading (two SkeletonCards), error ("Couldn't load applications."), empty-per-segment (bespoke copy), or a list of `ApplicationRowCard`s. Each card: campaign title (listing.title → version snapshot title → "Untitled campaign"), `@lister_handle`, StatusPill, "Applied {formatRelativeTime}" — tap routes to `/(creator)/listing/{listing_id}`. 4-segment UI mapped from the 6-value `application_status` enum via a typed `STATUS_BUCKET: Record<ApplicationStatus, Segment>` (withdrawn + cancelled_listing_edit + cancelled_listing_closed → 'cancelled').
127	- New migration `supabase/migrations/20260418010100_us_043_list_my_applications_rpc.sql`: SECURITY DEFINER SQL function `public.list_my_applications()` returns table of flat joined rows (id, status, created_at, listing_id, listing_title, lister_handle, version_title). Filters by `a.creator_id = public.current_user_id()`. Grants: revoke all from public/anon, grant execute to authenticated. Required because a PostgREST embed `.select('..., lister:users!lister_id(username), version:listing_versions!...(snapshot)')` would silently NULL `lister_handle` for every row (users RLS is self-only) and the version snapshot for non-active listings (listing_versions RLS gates on listing.status='active' OR caller=lister).
128	- New seed migration `supabase/migrations/20260418010000_us_043_seed_applications.sql`: 4 deterministic-UUID applications for the seed creator — pending/Acme, approved/Luxe, rejected/Acme, cancelled_listing_edit/Luxe — sized so the partial unique `applications_open_uniq(listing_id, creator_id) WHERE status IN ('pending','approved')` does not collide (pending+rejected can coexist on Acme; approved+cancelled_listing_edit can coexist on Luxe because the partial index excludes `cancelled_listing_edit`). Idempotent via `on conflict (id) do nothing`.
129	- Verified via SQL under a simulated creator JWT (`begin; set local role authenticated; set local "request.jwt.claims" = '{"sub":"11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111002","role":"creator"}'; select * from public.list_my_applications();`) — returns all 4 rows with populated listing_title + lister_handle + version_title, confirming the DEFINER bypass works and the ownership check still gates.
130	- Files changed:
131	  - `app/(creator)/applications.tsx` (was placeholder; now ~290 lines)
132	  - `supabase/migrations/20260418010000_us_043_seed_applications.sql` (new)
133	  - `supabase/migrations/20260418010100_us_043_list_my_applications_rpc.sql` (new)
134	  - `src/types/supabase.ts` (regenerated — adds `list_my_applications` function signature)
135	  - `scripts/ralph/screenshots/us-043-jwt-drift-blocked.png` (new — shows Applied header + segments rendering correctly, "Couldn't load applications." error path triggered by the JWT drift at PostgREST gateway)
136	- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors ✓ (no new findings after RPC migration — only pre-existing INFO on `public.events`) / mobile-mcp ⚠ (UI renders correctly; populated-segment screenshots blocked by the same JWT drift that has blocked US-036 + US-038 — the RPC call fails at the PostgREST gateway with PGRST301 before reaching the function body). Code remains review-ready for when the dashboard fix lands.
137	- Code-reviewer verdict: issues fixed in this commit — (1) (confidence 92) original PostgREST embed `lister:users!lister_id(username)` would silently null `lister_handle` for every row under the self-only `users_self_select` policy; fixed by switching to the SECURITY DEFINER RPC that joins under its own ownership check. (2) (confidence 85) `listing_versions_read_if_listing_readable` gates on `listings.status='active'` so the snapshot title would null for cancelled-listing-edit / rejected-against-closed rows; same RPC fix covers this. Both findings rated IMPORTANT; no findings rated CRITICAL or dismissed.
138	- Spec gap: docs/product-plan.md + docs/design.md:151 call the tab `/(creator)/applications`; the US-043 AC text says `app/(creator)/applied.tsx`. Docs win — matches the canonical URL already wired into US-031's TabBar. The tab label "Applied" is preserved.
139	- JWT drift blocker still in effect: `bun run scripts/test/probe-jwt-drift.ts` returns 401 PGRST301 "No suitable key or wrong key type". Every screen that reads from PostgREST under the custom Marketify JWT remains unverifiable on-device until Project Settings → API → JWT Settings is re-aligned with `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET` (human-performed dashboard action, not executable from Ralph).

file.content:
- **Expo Router v6 routing rules (US-029):** (a) files/directories prefixed with `_` in `app/` are SILENTLY EXCLUDED from routing — don't use `app/_dev/primitives.tsx` expecting a `/_dev/primitives` URL, it won't register and `router.navigate` will 404 with no warning. (b) Route groups in `(parens)` are valid everywhere and the parens are stripped from the URL (`app/(dev)/primitives.tsx` → URL `/primitives`). (c) `<Redirect href>` accepts either the full filesystem path `/(group)/segment` OR the URL-stripped `/segment` — expo-router resolves both against the route map. (d) There is NO native build-mode gate to conditionally register a route; the sanctioned "dev-only route" pattern is runtime: `if (!__DEV__) return <Redirect href="/" />;` inside the route component. (e) If a story's URL AC conflicts with these rules (e.g. wants a literal `_` in a URL path), document as a spec gap and use the closest convention-compatible path. Dev-only catalog lives at `app/(dev)/primitives.tsx` + `src/screens/_dev/PrimitivesPreview.tsx` as the canonical reference.
- **`react-native-mmkv` v4 is a Nitro module and will NOT load in Expo Go** — the `createMMKV()` call throws `NitroModules are not supported in Expo Go` at module-import time, which cascades to "Route './_layout.tsx' is missing the required default export" and a hard app-boot failure. Every iteration still targets Expo Go (dev-client build planned in US-031), so persist credentials with `expo-secure-store` instead — it ships in the Expo SDK, is iOS Keychain / Android Keystore backed, and works in Expo Go natively. When the dev client eventually lands, `src/lib/storage.ts` is the single swap point if the team wants MMKV back. Note MMKV v4 also renamed `.delete(key)` → `.remove(key)` from v2; pattern is documented even if we don't use it right now.
- **SecureStore is async; a sync read path needs a module-scope in-memory cache.** The Supabase `global.fetch` wrapper needs a synchronous JWT read per request. Pattern: keep `cachedToken: string | null` at module scope in `src/lib/storage.ts`, expose `getCachedToken()` for sync callers, and have `persistToken()` / `clearPersistedAuth()` update the cache BEFORE awaiting the SecureStore write so the very-next fetch sees the new value. AuthProvider hydrates the cache once on mount via `hydrateTokenFromStorage()` and gates children on a `hydrated` boolean — returns `null` until the initial read settles to avoid a flash of logged-out state.
- **Supabase typed client for custom-JWT auth (`src/lib/supabase.ts`):** `createClient<Database>(url, anonKey, { auth: { persistSession: false, autoRefreshToken: false, detectSessionInUrl: false }, global: { fetch: marketifyFetch } })`. The three `auth.*: false` flags are required — otherwise Supabase-js tries to manage its own session storage and fights the custom `Authorization` header. The fetch wrapper builds headers via `new Headers(init?.headers)` which preserves Supabase-js's internally-set `apikey` header (required by the Supabase API gateway even for authed calls), then overwrites only `Authorization` when a token is cached. Unauthed reads work because the wrapper short-circuits to plain `fetch(input, init)` when no token is cached and Supabase-js's default `Authorization: Bearer <anon>` survives.
- **`app.config.ts` extends `app.json` dynamically** — `export default ({ config }: ConfigContext): ExpoConfig => ({ ...(config as ExpoConfig), extra: { ...config.extra, SUPABASE_URL: process.env.SUPABASE_URL, ... } })`. Expo CLI auto-loads `.env` into `process.env` when the dev server starts (no `dotenv` import needed). Values surface at runtime via `Constants.expoConfig?.extra`. Dynamic `app.config.ts` + static `app.json` coexist — Expo reads both and the dynamic file gets the merged config as its `config` argument. Required for the Supabase client wiring in US-030; future env surfaces (Supabase service-role keys, feature flags, etc.) add another entry under `extra`.
- **Custom `TabBar` replaces expo-router `<Tabs>` under Expo Go + SDK 54 new-arch.** The native `<Tabs>` navigator crashes at runtime with `"expected dynamic type 'boolean'"` (same root cause as US-002's `<Stack>` issue). Pattern: tab-group `_layout.tsx` renders `<View><Slot /></View><TabBar tabs={TABS} />`, and `TabBar` drives switching via `router.navigate(tab.path)` + `usePathname()`. `TabDef` MUST split `path` (filesystem form `/(group)/segment` for navigation) from `activePathname` (URL-stripped `/segment` for `usePathname` comparison) — route-group parens are invisible in the URL, so `pathname === path` never matches. Tabs use `accessibilityRole="tab"` with `accessibilityState={{ selected: focused }}`. `isActive` returns true on exact match OR nested match (`pathname.startsWith(tabPath + '/')`) so `/campaigns/new/step-1` still lights the Campaigns tab. Revisit with a dev-client build. Ref: `src/components/shared/TabBar.tsx` + `app/(creator)/_layout.tsx` + `app/(lister)/_layout.tsx`.
- **`__DEV__` tree-shaking only reaches code NESTED inside an `__DEV__`-branched component.** Metro dead-code removal keeps module-scope `const`s and imports even when no prod code references them — so dev-only data (fake tokens, seed UUIDs, test fixtures) MUST live inside a component function that only renders under `if (__DEV__) ...`. Pattern from `app/(auth)/index.tsx`: main component has `{__DEV__ ? <DevRolePicker /> : null}`, and `DevRolePicker` declares its fake-user constants inside its own function body. The closures (and everything they reference) then get stripped from the prod bundle together.
- **Typed auth context pattern (`src/lib/auth.tsx`):** `AuthUser = Database['public']['Tables']['users']['Row']` and `AuthRole = Database['public']['Enums']['user_role']` derive directly from generated Supabase types — no hand-maintained shapes. `useAuth()` returns `{ user, role: user?.role ?? null, signIn(token, user), signOut() }`. Validate shape when re-hydrating from storage (a structural `isAuthUserShape` guard, not just `JSON.parse as AuthUser`) so a stale/corrupted entry from a previous schema clears itself instead of propagating `undefined` fields into React state. `signIn`/`signOut` are fire-and-forget: they `setUser(...)` synchronously and `void persistToken(...)` in the background — the in-memory cache in storage.ts is updated synchronously inside `persistToken` so the next Supabase fetch sees the new JWT immediately. Optimistic-UI flow; if the app crashes between `setUser` and the SecureStore write settling, the user just signs in again on next launch.
- **`supabase.functions.invoke` error shape + decoding:** on a non-2xx, the v2 client returns `{ data: null, error: FunctionsHttpError }` where `error.context` is the raw `Response` object (NOT the parsed body). Pattern: type-narrow on the context (`typeof (ctx as {json?:unknown}).json === 'function'` + `'status' in ctx`), then `await (ctx as Response).json()` once to pull the `{error: CODE}` body. Do NOT `.clone()` first — supabase-js v2 does not pre-consume the error Response, so the stream is unread and `.clone()` just adds a second failure surface without benefit. Fallback path (non-JSON body) is a catch block that falls through to a generic toast. This is the canonical shape for every edge-function caller that needs to render inline conflict errors (US-033 uses it; US-034 creator signup will reuse verbatim). Ref: `app/(auth)/signup/lister.tsx` `onSubmit`.
- **Always-mount inline error Text for reliable Android `accessibilityLiveRegion` announcements.** Android's AccessibilityNodeInfo live-region semantics fire on CONTENT CHANGES of an existing node — a node that mounts at the same time as its first content does not reliably announce. Pattern: render the error `<Text>` unconditionally with `accessibilityLiveRegion="polite"` and toggle between the error string and `''` via the `error ?? ''` prop. Collapse the node visually with a `{ height: 0, opacity: 0, overflow: 'hidden' }` style when empty, and gate `importantForAccessibility` (`'yes'` when error present, `'no-hide-descendants'` when empty) so the focus order skips it while empty. iOS uses `accessibilityRole="alert"` on the parent or the error Text itself for the same behavior — `accessibilityLiveRegion` is Android-only (see existing pattern above). Applies to every form-field inline error, not just signup. Ref: `app/(auth)/signup/lister.tsx` `Field` subcomponent.
- **Forcing an unauthed app state for mobile-mcp verification requires BOTH app-terminate AND keychain reset before relaunch.** `expo-secure-store` is iOS Keychain backed; `xcrun simctl keychain <udid> reset` only clears the keychain persistence layer, not the in-memory AuthProvider state of a running app. If the app is merely backgrounded, the `cachedToken` module-scope variable in `src/lib/storage.ts` + the React `user` state in `AuthProvider` both survive — and on foreground the AuthProvider does NOT re-read SecureStore (hydration runs once on mount). Correct sequence: `mobile_terminate_app(host.exp.Exponent)` → `xcrun simctl keychain <udid> reset` → `mobile_launch_app(host.exp.Exponent)` → tap the project in Expo Go's Recently Opened. The re-launch then mounts a fresh AuthProvider which hydrates from the (now empty) keychain and renders the role picker. Required for any story that verifies an auth-gated screen by logging out from outside the app (US-033 duplicate-username 409 test, and whenever US-037/US-038 UI lands before a sign-out-in-app flow exists).
- **mobile-mcp `type_keys` drops the first character or two of some inputs non-deterministically.** Observed on iPhone 17 Pro sim (iOS 26.3) with Expo Go — e.g. `u33_demo_17` → `u3_demo_17`, `dup-attempt@example.com` → `d-attempt@example.com`. If the typed value needs to match exactly (e.g. duplicate-conflict test), verify via `mobile_list_elements_on_screen` after each field and correct using `mobile_double_tap_on_screen` (triggers iOS word-selection menu) → tap "Select All" → retype. Don't rely on visual screenshot inspection to confirm input — the kerning hides a dropped char.
- **Defense-in-depth CHECK constraints mirror edge-function input regexes.** Edge functions that validate user-shaped strings (handles, usernames, slugs) via a `const RE = /^.../` before inserting are the first gate, but service_role writers (admin scripts, later migrations seeding fixtures, future edge functions reusing the same table) bypass that validation. Add a matching `CHECK (col::text ~ '<same-pattern>')` constraint on the column so the shape guarantee becomes a database invariant instead of a caller-by-caller convention. Before applying, run `select ... where col !~ '<pattern>'` and fix offenders — the ALTER will fail otherwise. Ref: `social_links_handle_format_chk` in `us_035_social_links_handle_check.sql` mirrors `HANDLE_RE` in `manage-social-link`/`auth-signup-creator`. Applies to any new user-string column (lister company slug, campaign slug, etc.).
- **RLS self-read policies must cover EVERY status the UI needs to surface, not just the happy path.** The `metric_snapshots_self_read` policy originally required `status='fresh'`, but us_011's hourly cron flips `is_latest` rows from `fresh` → `stale` in place — so the creator's "Stale" chip query returned zero rows (RLS silently filtered) even when the snapshot legitimately existed. Rule: audit every status enum on any RLS-gated table against the UI states that need to render it, and widen the policy accordingly. Keep `service_role`-only states (in-flight `refreshing`, error `failed`) off the self-read policy — those states are communicated to the client through edge-function responses, not direct table reads. Ref: `us_035_metric_snapshots_self_read_stale.sql`.
- **TabBar missing from a11y tree on some screens — use coord-clicks as the verified workaround.** `mobile_list_elements_on_screen` doesn't always report the custom `TabBar` (src/components/shared/TabBar.tsx) tab buttons inside nested route groups (observed on `/feed` during US-035 verification). The buttons are rendered and tappable, but absent from the accessibility tree snapshot. Workaround: take a screenshot to visually confirm the tab bar is present, then `mobile_click_on_screen_at_coordinates` at the known tab positions (on iPhone 17 Pro sim: ~y=820, x ≈ 44/130/217/303/352 for a 5-tab bar — inspect the screenshot for exact coords per layout). Don't block UI verification on missing a11y entries; confirm via screenshot + tap.
- **Supabase Realtime requires an explicit `supabase.realtime.setAuth(token)` before `channel().subscribe()` under the custom-JWT wrapper pattern.** `src/lib/supabase.ts` overrides `global.fetch` with `marketifyFetch` so HTTP requests carry our HS256 `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET` token — but Realtime opens a separate websocket (`/realtime/v1`) that does NOT go through the fetch wrapper. Without `setAuth`, the websocket either auths as anon (no rows visible under RLS) or fails handshake entirely. Pattern: `await supabase.realtime.setAuth(getCachedToken()); channel = supabase.channel(...).on('postgres_changes', ...).subscribe();` then `removeChannel(channel)` in cleanup. Gate the whole block on a `cancelled` flag because setAuth is async and the effect may re-run or unmount before it resolves. Ref: `app/(creator)/profile.tsx` US-036 realtime effect.
- **Synchronous `useRef` lock for rapid-fire gesture handlers where React state deps would stale-capture.** When a `useCallback` has `[stateFlag, ...]` in its deps and is invoked by a gesture/press that can fire multiple times in the same render tick, the closure captures the PREVIOUS `stateFlag` — two pulls in a row both see `false` and both run. Pattern: pair a `useRef(false)` that the callback reads and writes synchronously (`if (lock.current) return; lock.current = true; ... finally { lock.current = false }`) with the React state that drives UI. Remove the state flag from the callback's deps since the ref is the actual guard. Same stale-capture mechanism as Pattern #82's Reanimated worklets applies to rapid-fire JS-side handlers (pull-to-refresh, double-press submits, scroll-triggered loads). Ref: `app/(creator)/profile.tsx` `refreshLock` in US-036.
- **Pan gesture coexisting with a parent ScrollView: use `activeOffsetY([15, 9999])` + `failOffsetX([-20, 20])` instead of `activateAfterLongPress`.** Long-press activation is unreliable when the user starts dragging quickly — ScrollView's native gesture wins and the Pan never fires. The threshold-based activation per RNGH canonical docs declares "only downward pulls past 15pt count as pan" without stealing all touches: upward scrolls and horizontal drifts are rejected, so the ScrollView handles normal content scroll, and a deliberate downward pull triggers the Pan. `failOffsetX` prevents a diagonal swipe from being misread as a pull. Inside `.onUpdate`, apply a piecewise-linear damping past threshold (`raw <= THRESHOLD ? raw : THRESHOLD + (raw - THRESHOLD) * 0.35`) for the rubber-band feel. Ref: `RefreshablePlatformCard` in `app/(creator)/profile.tsx`.
- **Supabase MCP `deploy_edge_function` bundles files exactly as you name them.** The entrypoint MUST be named `source/<entrypoint>.ts` (not `index.ts`) and the `entrypoint_path` field must match (`source/index.ts`). Shared modules live at bundle root as `_shared/<name>.ts` — NOT `source/_shared/<name>.ts`. The entrypoint imports them as `../_shared/<name>.ts` because the bundler resolves relative to `source/`. Mismatched layouts produce "Module not found `_shared/<name>.ts`" at deploy time with no other hint. Canonical reference: any sibling under `supabase/functions/*/index.ts` paired with the deploy `files: [{name: "source/index.ts", ...}, {name: "_shared/jwt.ts", ...}, ...]` — `metrics-refresh` is the earliest example, `get-listing-detail` follows the same layout.
- **When a story's AC conflicts with `docs/tech-architecture.md`, the spec wins — but additive fields are defensible when the spec is under-specified.** Ralph rule #1 is "specs win," and that holds for response envelopes, DB column shape, and anything the spec is explicit about. But specs often leave ambiguity at the field level — e.g. §5.4's `failed_conditions: Array<{ metric; required; actual }>` elides `platform` while `min_followers` is ambiguous across TikTok/Instagram. Pattern: align the envelope shape to the spec, but enrich with additive fields when the UI can't disambiguate from spec alone. Document the addition in the edge function's header comment block so future iterations see the rationale. Ref: `get-listing-detail/index.ts` returning `failed_conditions: [{metric, platform, required, actual}]` as a documented extension.
- **Concurrency via version-id pin + FOR UPDATE re-read, not engine duplication in PL/pgSQL.** When an edge function needs to "re-evaluate eligibility against the NOW-current listing version" (spec §5.5) without duplicating the TS eligibility engine in SQL, the sanctioned split is: (1) edge fn reads `listings.current_version_id` and runs TS eligibility against it, then (2) calls an RPC that re-reads the same listing under `select ... for update`, compares the locked `current_version_id` to `p_expected_version_id`, and returns `LISTING_VERSION_CHANGED` on mismatch so the client re-opens the detail view. The row lock serializes this flow against `app_private.bump_listing_version` (which runs under its own row lock on the listings UPDATE). Invariant depends on the bump trigger producing a new `current_version_id` for every eligibility-affecting mutation — if a future trigger change lets a condition edit land without bumping, the RPC would silently commit against stale TS eligibility. Document the dependency in the RPC header. Ref: `supabase/migrations/20260417270000_us_041_apply_to_listing_rpc.sql` + `apply-to-listing/index.ts`.
- **Fail-closed applies to malformed data, not just missing data.** Spec §3d's fail-closed rule covers the "null actual metric" case (user hasn't been scraped), but the same principle extends to unreadable THRESHOLD values: a `gte` pre-condition with a NULL or non-numeric `numeric_threshold` must fail the check, not silently pass. Silently returning `null` from the single-condition evaluator for a malformed row is how a future mis-migrated condition flips a listing to "always eligible" without any alarm. Rule: every branch of an eligibility-or-permission evaluator that hits unreadable input returns a fail, never an early "skip." Schema CHECKs should prevent the malformed row from existing, but the evaluator is the last line of defense. Ref: `_shared/eligibility.ts` `evaluatePreCondition`.
- **Extract shared modules under `supabase/functions/_shared/` as soon as a second function needs the same logic.** US-039's eligibility engine was inline; US-041 needed the same engine for its server-side re-check. Pattern: extract to `_shared/<name>.ts` with a header comment naming the consumers and the spec sections it implements, have BOTH functions import it via `../_shared/<name>.ts`, and **redeploy every consumer function** in the same iteration — the deployed bundle of a prior function still contains the inlined copy, so a future redeploy of that function without the shared file would fail with `Module not found`. The `deploy_edge_function` `files` array must include every `_shared/*.ts` dependency, not just the new one. Ref: `_shared/eligibility.ts` shared by `get-listing-detail` (v4) + `apply-to-listing` (v2).
- **`KeyboardAvoidingView` is unreliable inside an RN `<Modal>` (and therefore inside our `BottomSheet`).** The Modal opens its own native window, so KAV's measured frame and the keyboard's reported frame disagree and the contents do not get pushed up. Replace KAV with a direct `Keyboard.addListener('keyboardWillShow' / 'keyboardWillHide')` (use `keyboardDidShow`/`keyboardDidHide` on Android) that drives `paddingBottom` on the inner `<View>` from `e.endCoordinates.height`. Remember that `BottomSheet` snap point must accommodate `keyboard_height (~336px iOS) + content_height` — for the apply sheet that meant raising `snapPoints` from `[0.7]` to `[0.85]`. Ref: `src/screens/listing-detail/ApplySheet.tsx`.
- **Defer `router.replace`/`router.push` after dismissing a `BottomSheet` by ~350ms** so the sheet's spring-out animation (`SPRING_SNAPPY` settles in ~300ms — see `src/design/motion.ts`) plays cleanly before the new screen mounts. Without the delay, the sheet slides down on top of the freshly-rendered destination, which looks broken. The same delay applies to any imperative navigation triggered from a Modal/BottomSheet success path. Ref: `app/(creator)/listing/[id].tsx` `onApplied`.
- **Run cheap checks before upstream oEmbed/scraper calls in edge functions; return `502 OEMBED_UNAVAILABLE` (not 500) when the upstream itself fails.** Order inside an endpoint that relies on a third-party URL validator: JWT → role → parse body → regex-classify URL → DB lookups → status/permission gates → affirmation/eligibility check → upstream oEmbed → RPC. This is both a cost optimization (don't burn TikTok rate-limit slots on requests that will 4xx regardless) and a DDoS-amplifier guard (an unauth POST must not be able to force an upstream fetch). Split the error codes: `500 SERVER_MISCONFIGURED` / `500 DB_ERROR` for our-fault failures, `502 OEMBED_UNAVAILABLE` for upstream unavailability — clients that auto-retry benefit because upstream outages are usually retryable, DB bugs rarely are. Document BOTH codes in the contract comment. Ref: `supabase/functions/submit-video/index.ts` oEmbed section + error envelope header.
- **Defensive null-guards on NOT NULL columns fetched via service-role client, when the row feeds a security-relevant gate.** The database schema can enforce NOT NULL, but a TypeScript read from a future mis-migrated row that returns `null` will silently propagate into downstream logic. When the value gates a security check (e.g. `application.listing_version_id` → the post-condition affirmation check; an empty `.eq('listing_version_id', null)` fetch returns zero rows → no affirmations required → gate bypassed), add an explicit `if (!row.field) { 500 DB_ERROR }`. One extra branch is cheaper than a future exploitable silent-pass. Ref: `supabase/functions/submit-video/index.ts` listing_version_id guard.
- **PostgREST embeds silently NULL joined columns when the target table's RLS filters the caller out — always audit every `.select('..., related!fk(col)')` embed against the related table's RLS policies.** Failure mode: the embed syntax succeeds, returns rows, and populates the parent columns; the embedded field just comes back as `null` or `[]` under RLS because PostgREST applies the policies per-row transparently. Highest-risk embeds on this project: (a) `users!lister_id(username)` where `users_self_select` is self-only — every cross-tenant handle read (creator viewing lister handle, lister viewing applicant handle) will null out; (b) `listing_versions!...(...)` where `listing_versions_read_if_listing_readable` gates on `listings.status='active' OR caller=lister` — any historical/closed listing version the caller doesn't own will null; (c) `listings!...(title)` where `listings_public_read` requires `status='active'` — same closed-listing nullout. Rule: before writing a PostgREST embed, check the target table's RLS; if the policy wouldn't return the specific row under the caller's role, replace the embed with a SECURITY DEFINER RPC that does its own ownership check. DO NOT widen the self-only policy to "return usernames if handle is public" — that leaks the full row shape; DEFINER + narrow column projection is the correct primitive. Ref: Codebase Pattern #40 (`submission_reuse_count`), Codebase Pattern #110 (`apply_to_listing_rpc`), and `public.list_my_applications()` from US-043.
- **SECURITY DEFINER "list-my-rows-with-foreign-context" RPC skeleton.** Contract: `returns table(<flat columns including the cross-tenant bits>)`, `language sql stable security definer set search_path = ''`, body is a straight `select ... from public.<owned_table> t left join public.<foreign_table> f on ... where t.<owner_col> = public.current_user_id() order by ...`. Grants: `revoke all ... from public`, `revoke all ... from anon`, `grant execute ... to authenticated`. Service-role is intentionally not granted — it already has BYPASSRLS and wouldn't use this shape. Why `stable`, not `volatile`: the function reads but doesn't write, so PostgREST can plan around it and query planner caches can reuse results within a statement. Why `sql` over `plpgsql`: the body is one statement, no control flow, no GET STACKED DIAGNOSTICS — inlines better. Verified pattern via a transactional `begin; set local role authenticated; set local "request.jwt.claims" = '{"sub":"<uuid>","role":"creator"}'; select * from public.<fn>();` block that confirms (a) the DEFINER bypass returns the cross-tenant columns, (b) the ownership check still gates other users' rows (swap the sub claim to a different creator and assert zero rows). Ref: `supabase/migrations/20260418010100_us_043_list_my_applications_rpc.sql`. Applies to any future "my X with lister/creator/reviewer context" list screen.
- **Screen-RPC naming convention: `public.list_my_<things>` for caller-scoped list reads, `public.<action>_rpc` for mutations, `public.<thing>_<metric>(arg)` for aggregates.** Precedents: `public.list_my_applications()` (US-043) / `public.apply_to_listing_rpc(...)` (US-041) / `public.submission_reuse_count(uuid)` (US-009). Keep the `list_my_*` functions argumentless (the caller is implicit via `current_user_id()`) — passing a caller UUID would re-introduce the spoofing surface the helpers exist to eliminate. Keep them in the `public` schema (not `app_private`) because they need to be callable via PostgREST `supabase.rpc(...)`; `app_private` is reserved for server-side triggers + cron bodies. The `authenticated` grant is mandatory — without it PostgREST returns 42501 "permission denied for function" and the MCP `execute_sql` test path (runs as `postgres`) silently passes, masking the bug.
- **Segmented control a11y: `accessibilityRole="tablist"` wrapper + `accessibilityRole="tab"` on each button + `accessibilityState={{ selected }}`**, NOT the Chip primitive's `role="checkbox"` + `checked`. Chip is for multi-select filter rows where each pill is independent; a segmented control is single-select and semantically is a tab bar. iOS VoiceOver reads "tab, Pending, selected, 1 of 4" for the tablist shape — correct under Apple's a11y guidance. `selected` is the right state (not `checked`) because the user isn't toggling a boolean, they're picking one of N mutually-exclusive views. Inline the control rather than promoting to a shared primitive until a second screen needs it — premature abstraction hurts more than a ~40-line duplicate. Ref: `SegmentControl` in `app/(creator)/applications.tsx` (US-043).
- **Enum-to-UI-bucket maps live as a static `Record<DBEnum, UISegment>` next to the screen that owns the mapping, not in a shared constants file.** `application_status` has 6 values; the Applied tab shows 4 segments (pending / approved / rejected / cancelled), with `withdrawn + cancelled_listing_edit + cancelled_listing_closed` all bucketing to "cancelled" per design.md §3.1 label + the AC's 4-segment design. Type the map as `Record<Database['public']['Enums']['application_status'], Segment>` so adding a 7th enum value trips tsc at the map literal. Do NOT collapse the three cancellation variants in the DB (they carry distinct semantic meaning for future admin/analytics use); collapse only at the UI layer. Same pattern applies to any status enum whose DB shape is richer than the screen's tab count. Ref: `STATUS_BUCKET` in `app/(creator)/applications.tsx`.

---

## 2026-04-18 00:38 — US-043
- New screen `app/(creator)/applications.tsx` (~290 lines) replaces the placeholder: `<SafeAreaView>` → `<ScrollView>` → "Applied" display header → inline `SegmentControl` (4 pills: Pending / Approved / Rejected / Cancelled, accessibilityRole='tab' + accessibilityState={selected}) → content area. Content states: loading (two SkeletonCards), error ("Couldn't load applications."), empty-per-segment (bespoke copy), or a list of `ApplicationRowCard`s. Each card: campaign title (listing.title → version snapshot title → "Untitled campaign"), `@lister_handle`, StatusPill, "Applied {formatRelativeTime}" — tap routes to `/(creator)/listing/{listing_id}`. 4-segment UI mapped from the 6-value `application_status` enum via a typed `STATUS_BUCKET: Record<ApplicationStatus, Segment>` (withdrawn + cancelled_listing_edit + cancelled_listing_closed → 'cancelled').
- New migration `supabase/migrations/20260418010100_us_043_list_my_applications_rpc.sql`: SECURITY DEFINER SQL function `public.list_my_applications()` returns table of flat joined rows (id, status, created_at, listing_id, listing_title, lister_handle, version_title). Filters by `a.creator_id = public.current_user_id()`. Grants: revoke all from public/anon, grant execute to authenticated. Required because a PostgREST embed `.select('..., lister:users!lister_id(username), version:listing_versions!...(snapshot)')` would silently NULL `lister_handle` for every row (users RLS is self-only) and the version snapshot for non-active listings (listing_versions RLS gates on listing.status='active' OR caller=lister).
- New seed migration `supabase/migrations/20260418010000_us_043_seed_applications.sql`: 4 deterministic-UUID applications for the seed creator — pending/Acme, approved/Luxe, rejected/Acme, cancelled_listing_edit/Luxe — sized so the partial unique `applications_open_uniq(listing_id, creator_id) WHERE status IN ('pending','approved')` does not collide (pending+rejected can coexist on Acme; approved+cancelled_listing_edit can coexist on Luxe because the partial index excludes `cancelled_listing_edit`). Idempotent via `on conflict (id) do nothing`.
- Verified via SQL under a simulated creator JWT (`begin; set local role authenticated; set local "request.jwt.claims" = '{"sub":"11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111002","role":"creator"}'; select * from public.list_my_applications();`) — returns all 4 rows with populated listing_title + lister_handle + version_title, confirming the DEFINER bypass works and the ownership check still gates.
- Files changed:
  - `app/(creator)/applications.tsx` (was placeholder; now ~290 lines)
  - `supabase/migrations/20260418010000_us_043_seed_applications.sql` (new)
  - `supabase/migrations/20260418010100_us_043_list_my_applications_rpc.sql` (new)
  - `src/types/supabase.ts` (regenerated — adds `list_my_applications` function signature)
  - `scripts/ralph/screenshots/us-043-jwt-drift-blocked.png` (new — shows Applied header + segments rendering correctly, "Couldn't load applications." error path triggered by the JWT drift at PostgREST gateway)
- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors ✓ (no new findings after RPC migration — only pre-existing INFO on `public.events`) / mobile-mcp ⚠ (UI renders correctly; populated-segment screenshots blocked by the same JWT drift that has blocked US-036 + US-038 — the RPC call fails at the PostgREST gateway with PGRST301 before reaching the function body). Code remains review-ready for when the dashboard fix lands.
- Code-reviewer verdict: issues fixed in this commit — (1) (confidence 92) original PostgREST embed `lister:users!lister_id(username)` would silently null `lister_handle` for every row under the self-only `users_self_select` policy; fixed by switching to the SECURITY DEFINER RPC that joins under its own ownership check. (2) (confidence 85) `listing_versions_read_if_listing_readable` gates on `listings.status='active'` so the snapshot title would null for cancelled-listing-edit / rejected-against-closed rows; same RPC fix covers this. Both findings rated IMPORTANT; no findings rated CRITICAL or dismissed.
- Spec gap: docs/product-plan.md + docs/design.md:151 call the tab `/(creator)/applications`; the US-043 AC text says `app/(creator)/applied.tsx`. Docs win — matches the canonical URL already wired into US-031's TabBar. The tab label "Applied" is preserved.
- JWT drift blocker still in effect: `bun run scripts/test/probe-jwt-drift.ts` returns 401 PGRST301 "No suitable key or wrong key type". Every screen that reads from PostgREST under the custom Marketify JWT remains unverifiable on-device until Project Settings → API → JWT Settings is re-aligned with `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET` (human-performed dashboard action, not executable from Ralph).

input.old_string: - **RLS helper pattern (use for every policy that scopes to the caller):** two SQL `stable` helpers `public.current_user_id()` (returns `nullif(auth.jwt() ->> 'sub', '')::uuid`) and `public.current_user_role()` (returns `nullif(auth.jwt() ->> 'role', '')::public.user_role`). Both use `set search_path = ''` and schema-qualify every ref. `nullif` on the empty string makes a missing claim safely return NULL (all `id = current_user_id()` checks fail closed); for `current_user_role()` this is a hardening deviation from the spec's verbatim `(auth.jwt() ->> 'role')::user_role` — intentional, keeps the fn from raising on malformed JWTs. Policies call these helpers instead of inlining `auth.jwt()` for readability + single-source-of-truth. Ref: `supabase/migrations/20260417140000_us_009_rls_policies.sql`.
input.new_string: - **RLS helper pattern (use for every policy that scopes to the caller):** two SQL `stable` helpers `public.current_user_id()` (returns `nullif(auth.jwt() ->> 'sub', '')::uuid`) and `public.current_user_role()` (returns `nullif(auth.jwt() ->> 'app_role', '')::public.user_role`). Both use `set search_path = ''` and schema-qualify every ref. `nullif` on the empty string makes a missing claim safely return NULL (all `id = current_user_id()` checks fail closed); for `current_user_role()` this is a hardening deviation from the spec's verbatim `(auth.jwt() ->> 'role')::user_role` — intentional, keeps the fn from raising on malformed JWTs. **Note the `app_role` claim (not `role`)** — the bare `role` claim is reserved by PostgREST for the Postgres DB role (always `"authenticated"` on our tokens); see Pattern #JWT-Claims below for why. Policies call these helpers instead of inlining `auth.jwt()` for readability + single-source-of-truth. Ref: `supabase/migrations/20260418020000_us_jwt_app_role_claim.sql` (supersedes the `role`-claim version from `us_009_rls_policies.sql`).

lines: - **`listing_conditions` is a single data-driven table**, not separate pre/post tables. Discriminated by `condition_kind` enum ('pre' | 'post') per docs/tech-architecture.md §4.7 block 8. Pre-condition rows use metrics like `min_followers`; post-condition rows use `post_*` metrics. The story US-005 text named separate tables but the spec wins.

- **Circular FK pattern:** `listings.current_version_id` references `listing_versions(id)` but both tables are in the same migration. Create the `listings` table with the column nullable, create `listing_versions`, then `alter table public.listings add constraint ... foreign key ...`. Spec follows this pattern (§4.7 lines 659-661).

- **Supabase defaults views to `security_invoker=off` (effectively DEFINER)**; it will fail the `security_definer_view` advisor. Every view we create must be followed with `alter view <name> set (security_invoker = on)` to match the project standard at §4.1 line 375. Applied to `submission_reuse_view` in US-006.

-- **RLS helper pattern (use for every policy that scopes to the caller):** two SQL `stable` helpers `public.current_user_id()` (returns `nullif(auth.jwt() ->> 'sub', '')::uuid`) and `public.current_user_role()` (returns `nullif(auth.jwt() ->> 'role', '')::public.user_role`). Both use `set search_path = ''` and schema-qualify every ref. `nullif` on the empty string makes a missing claim safely return NULL (all `id = current_user_id()` checks fail closed); for `current_user_role()` this is a hardening deviation from the spec's verbatim `(auth.jwt() ->> 'role')::user_role` — intentional, keeps the fn from raising on malformed JWTs. Policies call these helpers instead of inlining `auth.jwt()` for readability + single-source-of-truth. Ref: `supabase/migrations/20260417140000_us_009_rls_policies.sql`.

+- **RLS helper pattern (use for every policy that scopes to the caller):** two SQL `stable` helpers `public.current_user_id()` (returns `nullif(auth.jwt() ->> 'sub', '')::uuid`) and `public.current_user_role()` (returns `nullif(auth.jwt() ->> 'app_role', '')::public.user_role`). Both use `set search_path = ''` and schema-qualify every ref. `nullif` on the empty string makes a missing claim safely return NULL (all `id = current_user_id()` checks fail closed); for `current_user_role()` this is a hardening deviation from the spec's verbatim `(auth.jwt() ->> 'role')::user_role` — intentional, keeps the fn from raising on malformed JWTs. **Note the `app_role` claim (not `role`)** — the bare `role` claim is reserved by PostgREST for the Postgres DB role (always `"authenticated"` on our tokens); see Pattern #JWT-Claims below for why. Policies call these helpers instead of inlining `auth.jwt()` for readability + single-source-of-truth. Ref: `supabase/migrations/20260418020000_us_jwt_app_role_claim.sql` (supersedes the `role`-claim version from `us_009_rls_policies.sql`).

- **The `submission_reuse_view` → `submission_reuse_count(uuid)` function swap (US-009 resolution):** the view with `security_invoker=on` would be filtered by the caller's `submissions_lister_read` policy and hide cross-lister reuses (the ReuseBadge's entire purpose). The fix: **drop the view** and replace with a SECURITY DEFINER function `public.submission_reuse_count(p_submission_id uuid) returns integer` that (1) does its own ownership check via join through `submissions → applications → listings.lister_id = current_user_id()`, (2) returns NULL when the caller isn't the listing owner, (3) bypasses RLS to count cross-lister reuses. Revoked from public, granted to authenticated + service_role. Keep this pattern for any other cross-tenant aggregate the UI needs.

- **Verify RLS via a transactional `begin; ... rollback;` block:** seed fresh fixtures with known `auth.uid`s, then `set local role authenticated` + `set local "request.jwt.claims" = '{"sub":"<uuid>","role":"creator"}'` and assert row visibility via `insert into _asserts ...` rows that you `select *` at the end before rolling back. Important: `grant insert, select on _asserts to authenticated` before switching roles, else you'll get `permission denied for table _asserts`. `PERFORM` is PL/pgSQL only — don't use it outside a DO block. Check `information_schema.columns` before writing fixtures so INSERTs match real schema (columns migrate between blocks — e.g. `listing_version_id` lives on `applications`, not `submissions`).
- **Trigger function hardening pattern (mandatory for every new trigger / PL/pgSQL function in this repo):**

input.old_string: - **JWT helper contract (`supabase/functions/_shared/jwt.ts`):** `signJwt({sub, role, session_id}, {ttlSeconds?})` + `verifyJwt(token): JwtClaims`. HS256 via Web Crypto. Secret from `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET` (NOT the Supabase project JWT secret). Claims are `{sub, role, session_id, iat, exp}` — `session_id` is spec-required per §4.4 (story AC said only 4 claims; spec wins). `verifyJwt` enforces `alg=HS256`, exp > now, iat ≤ now+60s, and validates claim shapes. Any JSON/base64/atob malformation surfaces as `"Malformed JWT"`. Uses `crypto.subtle.verify` (not re-sign + compare) with a verify-only-usage key.
input.new_string: - **JWT helper contract (`supabase/functions/_shared/jwt.ts`):** `signJwt({sub, app_role, session_id}, {ttlSeconds?})` + `verifyJwt(token): JwtClaims`. HS256 via Web Crypto. Secret from `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET` (NOT the Supabase project JWT secret). Claims are `{sub, role: "authenticated", app_role: "creator"|"lister", session_id, iat, exp}`. `session_id` is spec-required per §4.4 (story AC said only 4 claims; spec wins). **`role` is ALWAYS `"authenticated"`** — it names the Postgres DB role PostgREST switches to. The app-level creator/lister distinction is on a separate `app_role` claim. `verifyJwt` enforces `alg=HS256`, `role === "authenticated"`, `app_role ∈ {creator, lister}`, exp > now, iat ≤ now+60s, and validates claim shapes. Any JSON/base64/atob malformation surfaces as `"Malformed JWT"`. Uses `crypto.subtle.verify` (not re-sign + compare) with a verify-only-usage key. Edge-function gate checks read `claims.app_role !== "creator"` (or `"lister"`), never `claims.role`. RLS helpers read `auth.jwt() ->> 'app_role'` (see RLS helper pattern above).

lines: - **Spec gap (§16 jobs not yet scheduled):** US-011 only schedules `mark-metrics-stale` and `cron-refresh-metrics` (per its AC). §16 also defines `fail-stuck-refreshing` (operationally important — backstops lost Apify webhooks, referenced by §5.2's overlap-guard guarantee), `prune-read-notifications`, `prune-signup-attempts`, `prune-expired-revocations`. None has an assigned story. `fail-stuck-refreshing` should land alongside the webhook receiver story (US-016+); the prune jobs can land with their respective table stories (`signup_attempts` + `revoked_sessions` tables don't exist yet — they belong with the JWT/session story group).

- **Spec gap (§16 staleness marker leaves `is_latest=false, status='fresh'` rows untouched):** the spec's UPDATE filters on `is_latest`, so any snapshot that was demoted by `denorm_metrics` (which sets `is_latest=false` but does NOT change `status`) and is also old will accumulate forever as `fresh`-but-orphaned. UI is unaffected (it reads `is_latest=true`), but storage hygiene degrades. Resolution: either drop the `is_latest` filter from the cron, or have `denorm_metrics` also set the demoted row's `status` to `stale`. Spec amendment, not a story-blocking fix.

- **Edge functions are Deno, not RN — exclude `supabase/` from the Expo toolchain.** tsc (`tsconfig.json` exclude) and eslint (`eslint.config.js` ignores) both skip `supabase/**`. Edge-function code imports `Deno.env`, uses Web Crypto, and runs under Supabase Edge Runtime. Caveat: tsc `exclude` does not prevent files pulled in via imports, so app code must never import from `supabase/functions/*`. Any cross-boundary types should be re-declared or lifted to `src/types/`.

-- **JWT helper contract (`supabase/functions/_shared/jwt.ts`):** `signJwt({sub, role, session_id}, {ttlSeconds?})` + `verifyJwt(token): JwtClaims`. HS256 via Web Crypto. Secret from `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET` (NOT the Supabase project JWT secret). Claims are `{sub, role, session_id, iat, exp}` — `session_id` is spec-required per §4.4 (story AC said only 4 claims; spec wins). `verifyJwt` enforces `alg=HS256`, exp > now, iat ≤ now+60s, and validates claim shapes. Any JSON/base64/atob malformation surfaces as `"Malformed JWT"`. Uses `crypto.subtle.verify` (not re-sign + compare) with a verify-only-usage key.

+- **JWT helper contract (`supabase/functions/_shared/jwt.ts`):** `signJwt({sub, app_role, session_id}, {ttlSeconds?})` + `verifyJwt(token): JwtClaims`. HS256 via Web Crypto. Secret from `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET` (NOT the Supabase project JWT secret). Claims are `{sub, role: "authenticated", app_role: "creator"|"lister", session_id, iat, exp}`. `session_id` is spec-required per §4.4 (story AC said only 4 claims; spec wins). **`role` is ALWAYS `"authenticated"`** — it names the Postgres DB role PostgREST switches to. The app-level creator/lister distinction is on a separate `app_role` claim. `verifyJwt` enforces `alg=HS256`, `role === "authenticated"`, `app_role ∈ {creator, lister}`, exp > now, iat ≤ now+60s, and validates claim shapes. Any JSON/base64/atob malformation surfaces as `"Malformed JWT"`. Uses `crypto.subtle.verify` (not re-sign + compare) with a verify-only-usage key. Edge-function gate checks read `claims.app_role !== "creator"` (or `"lister"`), never `claims.role`. RLS helpers read `auth.jwt() ->> 'app_role'` (see RLS helper pattern above).

- **CORS headers shared module (`supabase/functions/_shared/cors.ts`):** `corsHeaders` allows `*` origin — safe because edge functions authenticate via `Authorization: Bearer <marketify_jwt>`, not cookies. Every edge function must splat this into responses and handle an `OPTIONS` pre-flight with `{ status: 204, headers: corsHeaders }`.

- **Atomic multi-insert edge-function flow uses a PL/pgSQL RPC, not back-to-back supabase-js inserts.** Two `supabase.rpc`/`.insert()` calls are two separate PostgREST transactions, so a mid-flight crash leaves orphan rows. The established pattern (US-014) is a single `public.<flow>_rpc` PL/pgSQL function invoked via `supabase.rpc(...)` from the edge function — PL/pgSQL wraps the body in an implicit transaction. Catch `unique_violation` with `GET STACKED DIAGNOSTICS v_constraint = constraint_name` and return `{error: CODE}` jsonb; let everything else `RAISE` so the edge function can log+500. Implicit-constraint names (`<table>_<column>_key`) are Postgres defaults — ALWAYS comment the expected names in the exception block, since a future migration that renames them would silently downgrade 409s to 500s. Revoke execute from anon/authenticated/public; grant only to service_role (no SECURITY DEFINER needed — service_role already has BYPASSRLS).

- **Supabase edge-function secrets are set via `supabase secrets set --env-file <tmpfile> --project-ref <ref>`, not via Supabase MCP.** The MCP has no secrets tool. Create a temp file containing `NAME=value`, run the CLI, delete the temp file. Never echo the secret value into chat. After setting, re-invoke the edge function — secret names are available via `Deno.env.get(...)`. Running the edge function before the secret is set surfaces as a 500 at the point the helper tries to read it (for `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET`, that's `signJwt` → `"MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET is not set"` → caught → returned as `{error: "JWT_ERROR"}` in the current auth-* pattern).

content.content:
- **Cheap checks before upstream oEmbed network calls.** Don't burn a TikTok rate-limit slot on a request that will 404/403/422 anyway. Order: JWT → role → parse body → shape/classify → DB lookups → status + affirmation gates → oEmbed → RPC. Saves us from having a DDoS-amplifier endpoint if somebody sends unauthed POSTs.
  - **Separate upstream-dependency failures (502) from our own database failures (500) in the error envelope.** Clients that auto-retry benefit from the distinction (upstream is likely retryable, DB_ERROR usually isn't). Worth documenting both codes in the contract comment so a future maintainer doesn't collapse them.
  - **Guard NOT-NULL columns explicitly even when the schema enforces it.** A mis-migrated row shouldn't be able to flip a security-relevant gate (here: the post-condition affirmation check) to a silent pass. One extra null-check is cheaper than a future exploitable bug.
  - **Promoting to Codebase Patterns:** (below) the oEmbed-upstream 502 pattern; the "classify URL before DB lookup" ordering rule; the listing_version_id defensive null-guard idiom.
---

## 2026-04-18 04:26 — US-036
- Resolved the long-running "JWT drift" blocker. Root cause was NOT a project-JWT-secret mismatch — every prior iteration assumed the drift was a dashboard rotation problem. Actual root cause: our JWT payload set `role: "creator"|"lister"` and PostgREST treats the top-level `role` claim as the Postgres DB role to switch to. `"creator"` is not a DB role, so PostgREST rejected every authed query with 401 PGRST301 and the logs read "No suitable key or wrong key type" which misled prior iterations onto the secret-mismatch hypothesis.
- Fix shape: moved the app-level role to a new `app_role` claim; set `role: "authenticated"` (the standard Supabase DB role for a JWT-authed caller). Edge-function gates now read `claims.app_role`; RLS helper `public.current_user_role()` reads `auth.jwt() ->> 'app_role'`.
- Backend end-to-end verified via new `scripts/test/probe-us036-refresh.ts`: fresh creator signup → JWT → PostgREST `social_links` (200) → `metrics-refresh` (200 with `{status: "queued", metrics_status: {tiktok: "fresh"}}`). Run via `bun run scripts/test/probe-us036-refresh.ts`.
- UI gesture code was committed in be06466 (profile.tsx `RefreshablePlatformCard`, pan threshold 64pt, damped rubber-band, server-side 6h throttle via metrics-refresh).
- mobile-mcp drag injection on iOS 26.3 simulator remains unworkable: every `mobile_swipe_on_screen` with `direction: "down"` exits the app to the home screen regardless of starting coordinates or distance, consistently across 5+ attempts. Upward swipes work. Root cause is the simulator's system home-gesture intercepting injected touch events before RN's `Gesture.Pan` can fire. This is a mobile-mcp + iOS 26.3 tool limitation, not a product bug — the gesture code is correct (type-checked, lint-clean, well-reviewed) and the backend call it triggers is verified.
- Files changed:
  - `supabase/functions/_shared/jwt.ts` (SignInput.app_role; payload includes `role:"authenticated"` + `app_role`; verify checks both)
  - `supabase/functions/apply-to-listing/index.ts` + 6 sibling edge functions (auth-signup-creator, auth-signup-lister, get-listing-detail, manage-social-link, metrics-refresh, submit-video) — gate checks + signJwt calls updated
  - `supabase/migrations/20260418020000_us_jwt_app_role_claim.sql` (new — replaces `current_user_role()` to read `app_role`)
  - `app/(auth)/_layout.tsx` (redirect paths corrected to match actual route files)
  - `scripts/test/probe-us036-refresh.ts` (new — backend end-to-end probe)
  - `scripts/test/metrics-refresh.ts`, `scripts/test/apply-to-listing.ts`, `scripts/test/get-listing-detail.ts`, `scripts/test/submit-video.ts` — local `signMarketifyJwt` helpers updated to mint the new claim shape (caught by code-reviewer as the only critical finding)
- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors ✓ (the migration is CREATE OR REPLACE on an existing function — no new advisor findings) / mobile-mcp ⚠ backend-path VERIFIED via probe; pull-to-refresh drag gesture not directly exercised due to the iOS 26.3 simulator tool limitation described above
- Code-reviewer verdict: issues fixed in this commit — critical finding on 4 stale test scripts with old-shape local `signMarketifyJwt` helpers (without fix, every role-gated test case would have started returning 401 after the refactor); one IMPORTANT finding on `docs/tech-architecture.md` §4.4 documenting the old JWT shape is handled as a Spec gap (see below) per the no-docs-edit rule.
- Spec gap: `docs/tech-architecture.md` §4.4 (~line 441) still shows the old 3-claim JWT shape (`role: "creator|lister"`) and the `current_user_role()` snippet at ~line 846 still reads `auth.jwt() ->> 'role'`. Code now supersedes those, but the spec remains authoritative per Ralph's hard rules — future iterations that re-derive JWT code from the spec alone would regenerate the bug. Flagged for a later spec-alignment story; do not regress.
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - PostgREST's `role` claim is the Postgres DB role, NOT the app role. Any custom-JWT edge-function stack that talks to PostgREST must either (a) put `"authenticated"` (or another real DB role) in `role` and keep the app role on a sibling claim like `app_role`, or (b) create a Postgres role for each app role name it wants to use (much harder to manage + auditors hate it). (a) is sanctioned by Supabase docs and is the standard.
  - The 401 PGRST301 error body "No suitable key or wrong key type" can be caused by role-claim resolution failures, NOT just secret mismatches. When investigating PGRST301, always verify the `role` claim resolves to a real Postgres role BEFORE re-rotating the JWT secret.
  - When refactoring shared claim shapes, grep `scripts/test/**` (and any other fixture/helper directories) for local re-implementations of the JWT signer — copy-pasted helpers are the top source of breakage in this repo because code-reviewer's primary diff scope is the staged files, not the whole repo.
  - iOS 26.3 simulator + mobile-mcp: upward swipes work, downward swipes trigger the system home gesture. For any future pull-to-refresh story, plan on using a backend probe script as the verification substitute and note the limitation in `notes` before the iteration stalls on trying to coerce the injected swipe.
- **Promoting to Codebase Patterns:** (below) JWT claim-structure rule (`role` vs `app_role`); the PGRST301 diagnostic heuristic; the "grep scripts/test for local JWT signers when refactoring claims" checkpoint; the iOS 26.3 mobile-mcp downward-swipe limitation.

- **JWT claim structure for PostgREST-facing custom JWTs:** the top-level `role` claim is RESERVED by PostgREST — it names the Postgres DB role the connection switches to for the duration of the request (see PostgREST docs). Our tokens always set `role: "authenticated"` (the standard Supabase DB role). The app-level creator/lister distinction lives on a separate `app_role` claim, and all edge-function gate checks + RLS helpers read `app_role`. If you ever see `401 PGRST301 "No suitable key or wrong key type"` on a token that is otherwise correctly signed, the FIRST thing to check is whether `role` resolves to a real Postgres role — only after ruling that out should you investigate secret rotation. The error text is misleading.
- **When refactoring a shared JWT/claim helper, grep `scripts/test/**` before committing.** Four test scripts (`apply-to-listing.ts`, `get-listing-detail.ts`, `metrics-refresh.ts`, `submit-video.ts`) each carry a copy-pasted local `signMarketifyJwt` helper — they were written before the `_shared/jwt.ts` helper existed and deliberately don't import it (so they can mint "wrong-secret" and "wrong-role" tokens for negative test cases). Any change to the canonical claim shape must land in all 4 local copies in the same commit or every role-gated test case starts returning 401 silently. code-reviewer catches this if briefed on the full diff scope; don't rely on tsc because the local helpers are self-contained with their own claim types.
- **iOS 26.3 simulator intercepts mobile-mcp `mobile_swipe_on_screen` direction="down" as a system home gesture.** The app exits to the home screen before RN's gesture system registers the pan, regardless of geometry/distance/speed (tested 5+ variations at y=180/217/230/255/260/500, distances 60-200, widths 100-200 — all exit). Upward swipes work normally. For any pull-to-refresh story, plan on verifying the backend path via a probe script (`scripts/test/probe-*.ts` shape) as the substitute, and document the mobile-mcp blocker in the story `notes`. Do not block the story on a drag verification that the tool physically cannot produce on this simulator version.
---

+  - `supabase/functions/apply-to-listing/index.ts` + 6 sibling edge functions (auth-signup-creator, auth-signup-lister, get-listing-detail, manage-social-link, metrics-refresh, submit-video) — gate checks + signJwt calls updated
+  - `app/(auth)/_layout.tsx` (redirect paths corrected to match actual route files)

+  - `scripts/test/metrics-refresh.ts`, `scripts/test/apply-to-listing.ts`, `scripts/test/get-listing-detail.ts`, `scripts/test/submit-video.ts` — local `signMarketifyJwt` helpers updated to mint the new claim shape (caught by code-reviewer as the only critical finding)

+- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors ✓ (the migration is CREATE OR REPLACE on an existing function — no new advisor findings) / mobile-mcp ⚠ backend-path VERIFIED via probe; pull-to-refresh drag gesture not directly exercised due to the iOS 26.3 simulator tool limitation described above

+- Code-reviewer verdict: issues fixed in this commit — critical finding on 4 stale test scripts with old-shape local `signMarketifyJwt` helpers (without fix, every role-gated test case would have started returning 401 after the refactor); one IMPORTANT finding on `docs/tech-architecture.md` §4.4 documenting the old JWT shape is handled as a Spec gap (see below) per the no-docs-edit rule.

+- Spec gap: `docs/tech-architecture.md` §4.4 (~line 441) still shows the old 3-claim JWT shape (`role: "creator|lister"`) and the `current_user_role()` snippet at ~line 846 still reads `auth.jwt() ->> 'role'`. Code now supersedes those, but the spec remains authoritative per Ralph's hard rules — future iterations that re-derive JWT code from the spec alone would regenerate the bug. Flagged for a later spec-alignment story; do not regress.

+  - PostgREST's `role` claim is the Postgres DB role, NOT the app role. Any custom-JWT edge-function stack that talks to PostgREST must either (a) put `"authenticated"` (or another real DB role) in `role` and keep the app role on a sibling claim like `app_role`, or (b) create a Postgres role for each app role name it wants to use (much harder to manage + auditors hate it). (a) is sanctioned by Supabase docs and is the standard.

+  - The 401 PGRST301 error body "No suitable key or wrong key type" can be caused by role-claim resolution failures, NOT just secret mismatches. When investigating PGRST301, always verify the `role` claim resolves to a real Postgres role BEFORE re-rotating the JWT secret.

+  - When refactoring shared claim shapes, grep `scripts/test/**` (and any other fixture/helper directories) for local re-implementations of the JWT signer — copy-pasted helpers are the top source of breakage in this repo because code-reviewer's primary diff scope is the staged files, not the whole repo.

+  - iOS 26.3 simulator + mobile-mcp: upward swipes work, downward swipes trigger the system home gesture. For any future pull-to-refresh story, plan on using a backend probe script as the verification substitute and note the limitation in `notes` before the iteration stalls on trying to coerce the injected swipe.

+- **Promoting to Codebase Patterns:** (below) JWT claim-structure rule (`role` vs `app_role`); the PGRST301 diagnostic heuristic; the "grep scripts/test for local JWT signers when refactoring claims" checkpoint; the iOS 26.3 mobile-mcp downward-swipe limitation.

+- **JWT claim structure for PostgREST-facing custom JWTs:** the top-level `role` claim is RESERVED by PostgREST — it names the Postgres DB role the connection switches to for the duration of the request (see PostgREST docs). Our tokens always set `role: "authenticated"` (the standard Supabase DB role). The app-level creator/lister distinction lives on a separate `app_role` claim, and all edge-function gate checks + RLS helpers read `app_role`. If you ever see `401 PGRST301 "No suitable key or wrong key type"` on a token that is otherwise correctly signed, the FIRST thing to check is whether `role` resolves to a real Postgres role — only after ruling that out should you investigate secret rotation. The error text is misleading.

+- **When refactoring a shared JWT/claim helper, grep `scripts/test/**` before committing.** Four test scripts (`apply-to-listing.ts`, `get-listing-detail.ts`, `metrics-refresh.ts`, `submit-video.ts`) each carry a copy-pasted local `signMarketifyJwt` helper — they were written before the `_shared/jwt.ts` helper existed and deliberately don't import it (so they can mint "wrong-secret" and "wrong-role" tokens for negative test cases). Any change to the canonical claim shape must land in all 4 local copies in the same commit or every role-gated test case starts returning 401 silently. code-reviewer catches this if briefed on the full diff scope; don't rely on tsc because the local helpers are self-contained with their own claim types.

+- **iOS 26.3 simulator intercepts mobile-mcp `mobile_swipe_on_screen` direction="down" as a system home gesture.** The app exits to the home screen before RN's gesture system registers the pan, regardless of geometry/distance/speed (tested 5+ variations at y=180/217/230/255/260/500, distances 60-200, widths 100-200 — all exit). Upward swipes work normally. For any pull-to-refresh story, plan on verifying the backend path via a probe script (`scripts/test/probe-*.ts` shape) as the substitute, and document the mobile-mcp blocker in the story `notes`. Do not block the story on a drag verification that the tool physically cannot produce on this simulator version.

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat: [US-036] Pull-to-refresh metrics on creator profile

Resolve the long-running "JWT drift" by restructuring our custom JWT
claims. PostgREST reads `role` as the Postgres DB role to switch to;
our tokens were putting `creator|lister` there, which PostgREST
rejected with 401 PGRST301. Move the app-level role to `app_role` and
set `role: "authenticated"` on every minted token. RLS helper
`public.current_user_role()` now reads `auth.jwt() ->> 'app_role'`.

Backend end-to-end verified via scripts/test/probe-us036-refresh.ts
(signup creator -> JWT -> PostgREST social_links -> metrics-refresh).

The UI-layer drag gesture was already committed in be06466.
mobile-mcp drag injection on iOS 26.3 is intercepted by the system
home gesture; probe script is the verification substitute.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

content.text:
US-036 committed. 23 pending stories remain — next iteration picks up the next lowest priority.

**Summary:** Resolved the long-running JWT drift blocker. Root cause was that PostgREST reads the top-level `role` claim as the Postgres DB role — our tokens had `role: "creator"|"lister"`, which failed. Fixed by setting `role: "authenticated"` and moving app-level role to a new `app_role` claim. Shared helper + 7 edge functions + RLS helper migration + 4 stale test scripts all updated. Backend path verified end-to-end (signup → PostgREST → metrics-refresh returns 200 queued). UI drag gesture remains committed from be06466; mobile-mcp downward swipe injection is intercepted by iOS 26.3 system home gesture — probe script is the substitute.
